rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Receipts totals from the prior two accountings include unidentified deposits or property tax refunds that are not includable in the statutory fee calculation. (2) The attorney's fee declaration was not signed and does not fully meet the requirements of CRC 7.702. (3) The amendment was not e-filed until 3/28/25. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (4) The two accounts into which the executor has transferred funds total $3,505,694.18, not the $4,728,191.97 shown on hand at the end of the accounting. (5) The courtesy copy of the petition that was provided to the examiner was apparently not a true and complete copy of the pleading; there are numerous exhibits and pages of the accounting out of order or possibly missing. Please provide a revised proposed order (if appropriate) and a true and complete courtesy copy of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/20/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To List And Sell Conservatees Former Residence
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine whether the petitioning conservator demonstrated a compelling need to sell the conservatee's former personal residence for the benefit of the conservatee by clear and convincing evidence required by Probate Code section 2541.5, in light of the conservator's supplement admitting that there is currently no compelling need to sell and that the conservatee's needs continue to be provided for by Medi-Cal without selling the property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] 2. The prayer only requests that this matter be set for hearing and the [amended] report be approved; there is no mention of the extension of time to administer the estate. 3. It is unclear why a Corrected I&A cannot be filed promptly just removing the account that should not have been included, or why the accounting cannot be filed with an explanation, supported by evidence, that the inventoried account was not the decedent's, after all. Deadline for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 5/28/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIN YEE POON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the complete legal description, street address (if any), and assessor's parcel number (if any) of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) A brokerage account is not ordinarily all cash. Securities must be appraised by the probate referee. Please file a copy of the Fidelity Investments account immediately preceding the date of death. (3) If cash was invested in interest-bearing accounts, why is the balance exactly the same as on the date of death, 11/22/22? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/20/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILY C. TIMLOW TRUST FBO RICHARD CRAIG HILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Co-Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although neither proof of service filed on 12/23/24 or on 3/17/25 showed the correct caption of the SECOND AMENDED PETITION, as supplemented, that was to be heard. (Supplements ordinarily do not require notice. [CRC 7.53(b).] Petitions, amendments to petitions, or fully amended petitions do require notice.) Please do NOT attach printouts of tentative rulings to filed pleadings; they are not part of the record unless a party or attorney attaches them to a filed pleading. This is especially not appropriate where other cases, including confidential cases, are included in the printout. The Court is aware of what the issues are without having the tentative rulings printed out for it.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2040 Fell Street, Unit No. 10, San Francisco, Ca 94117)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following uncleared issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/08/2025): 1) Notice must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-020 (instead of DE-120 form on file). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the GC-020 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Notice must be given to each purchaser, absent a signed waiver of notice. 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) No inventory and appraisal is on file. 6) No reappraisal is on file. 7) Please supplement the petition in response to the following: i) Item 4c must specify the date of sale. ii) Please address the sufficiency of the bond, by stating the total personal assets on hand and estimated net proceeds from the sale. iii) Is the correct block number (APN) 1212 instead of 1210? iv) Please provide each purchaser's street address, absent a signed waiver of notice. Aside from those procedural issues, the judicial officer may discuss with counsel and determine: A) Whether to deny the conservator's ex parte application filed on 03/28/2025 for confirmation of the same sale, as the Court cannot grant the relief without going through the overbid process required by statute. B) Whether a petition under Probate Code section 2592 should be entertained if the conservator decides to request independent powers to sell the subject real property without court confirmation (see section 2591(c)) to avoid any overbid and new sale. C) Whether a petition for a waiver of the probate referee's appraisal (see sections 8903 & 2100) should be entertained if the referee has not received an inventory and appraisal and is unwilling to appraise the property based on the BMR property requirements imposed by the city. D) Whether counsel wants to proceed with court confirmation by clearing the outstanding issues and accepting a risk that, in the event of any overbid, the Court may have no choice but to deny confirmation and order a new sale under section 10311(c) to accommodate the city's requirements applicable to BMR property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANIE KATHLEEN LINDSAY BRAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to petitioner on 01/06/2025 & subsequently posted as tentative rulings for 02/19/2025 & 03/11/2025 hearings): 1) The proof of publication on file does not show the petitioner's request for IAEA authority was published. New notice must be published if the petitioner continues to seek IAEA authority (see item 2c of the petition requesting full authority). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 3) No DE-147 form is on file. 4) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) Item 2d(2) of the petition: The bond amount must be alleged. For full IAEA authority, the amount must match the amount alleged at item 3d(7). 7) Item 3a: Was the decedent domiciled in San Francisco County, despite her place of death in Burlingame, San Mateo County? 8) Item 3d(5): Is any real property subject to encumbrances (e.g., mortgage, line of credit, other lien)? If so, the total balance must be alleged. 9) Item 5a(3) or (4) (at least one box) must be checked. 10) Item 5a(7) or (8) (at least one box) must be checked. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The date of death of any deceased heir must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] (3) A written declination must be filed by or on behalf of an individual who has higher priority than the petitioner for issuance of letters of administration but does not desire to act. If the necessary renunciation or declination is not filed, the petition must indicate the reason. [LRSF 14.24.] (4) "Nominated Administrator" is not a relationship to the decedent. If the petitioner is not kin, he was a âstrangerâ to the decedent. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/28/25. (A supplement prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner should be sufficient to which the declinations by the other heirs are attached.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
